title: Automated design tool
abstract: A computer-implemented method of selecting design parameters for a system being designed includes developing, by a computing device an inverse model that includes a probabilistic representation of a simulation of a physical behavior of the system. The inverse model includes observable parameters and unobservable parameters. The method also includes specifying a desired value for a desired response characteristic for the system, specifying values for a portion of the observable parameters, and predicting, by an inverse model that includes a trained neural network, values of the observable parameters not specified and the unobservable parameters that result in the desired response characteristic equaling the desired value.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A computer-implemented method of selecting design parameters for a system being designed, the method comprising: developing, by a computing device an inverse model that includes a probabilistic representation of a simulation of a physical behavior of the system, the inverse model including observable parameters and unobservable parameters; specifying a desired value for a desired response characteristic for the system; specifying values for a portion of the observable parameters; and predicting, by the inverse model that includes a trained neural network, values of the observable parameters not specified and the unobservable parameters that result in the desired response characteristic equaling the desired value under consideration of uncertainties. 2. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, wherein the physical behavior comprises one of a temperature, a stress level, a strain level, and a displacement. 3. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, wherein the desired value includes one of a value range, a maximum allowable value, and a minimum required value. 4. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, wherein the system includes a combustion turbine engine. 5. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, wherein the trained neural network includes a Bayesian neural network. 6. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, further comprising operating an analysis tool to generate a plurality of field responses, each field response including values for the observable parameters, the unobservable parameters, and the values indicative of the physical behavior of the system being designed; and using the plurality of field responses to train the neural network. 7. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, further comprising creating, by a computing device, a vectorized model of the system being designed. 8. A computer-implemented method of selecting design parameters for a system being designed, the method comprising: developing, by a computing device an inverse model that includes a probabilistic representation of a temperature distribution of the system, the inverse model including observable parameters and unobservable parameters; specifying a desired temperature limit at a particular location within the system; specifying values for a portion of the observable parameters; and predicting, by the inverse model that includes a trained neural network, values of the observable parameters not specified and the unobservable parameters that result in the desired temperature limit at the particular location within the system. 9. The computer-implemented method of claim 8, wherein the desired temperature limit is a maximum temperature at any point within the system. 10. The computer-implemented method of claim 8, wherein the desired value includes one of a value range, a maximum allowable value, and a minimum required value. 11. The computer-implemented method of claim 8, wherein the system includes a combustion turbine engine. 12. The computer-implemented method of claim 8, wherein the trained neural network includes a Bayesian neural network. 13. The computer-implemented method of claim 8, further comprising operating a thermal analysis tool to generate a plurality of field responses, each field response including values for the observable parameters, the unobservable parameters, and the values indicative of the temperature of the system being designed; and using the plurality of field responses to train the neural network. 14. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, further comprising creating, by a computing device, a vectorized model of the system being designed. 15. A computing apparatus comprising: a processor; and a memory storing instructions that, when executed by the processor, configure the apparatus to: develop, by a computing device an inverse model that includes a probabilistic representation of a simulation of a physical behavior of the system, the inverse model including observable parameters and unobservable parameters; specify a desired value for a desired response characteristic for the system; specify values for a portion of the observable parameters; and predict, by the inverse model that includes a trained neural network, values of the observable parameters not specified and the unobservable parameters that result in the desired response characteristic equaling the desired value. 16. The apparatus of claim 15, wherein the physical behavior comprises one of a temperature, a stress level, and a strain level. 17. The apparatus of claim 15, wherein the desired value includes one of a value range, a maximum allowable value, and a minimum required value. 18. The apparatus of claim 15, apparatus of claim 15, wherein the system includes a combustion turbine engine. 19. The apparatus of claim 15, wherein the trained neural network includes a Bayesian neural network. 20. The computer-implemented method of claim 15, wherein the apparatus is further configured to operate an analysis tool to generate a plurality of field responses, each field response including values for the observable parameters, the unobservable parameters, and the values indicative of the physical behavior of the system being designed; and use the plurality of field responses to train the neural network.
